content:
Recursive Storytelling: SYSTEM specs narrate their own change history and include reasons for deprecations or new rules. Each major change is framed as a narrative turn, not just a diff.

Example name:
SYSTEM-ARCHIVE-SPEC-ROOT-0001-v1_0

1.4 WORLD Layer

Function: defines individual “worlds” across three dimensions: conceptual worlds (ontology and symbol systems), narrative worlds (characters, locations, timelines), and operational worlds (project ecosystems and pipelines).

Metaformeme: each world has a “seed phrase” and a “metaformeme cluster” that defines its core vocabulary. These clusters are reused whenever that world is touched; future AI runs are instructed to stick to the cluster or explicitly propose additions.

Myth Fusion: worlds are mythic domains with genealogies. Characters, agents, and modules within a world inherit traits from parent arcs or pantheons, which can be used as constraints or generative prompts.

Wave Theory: each world has internal waves: narrative arcs, release cycles, and operational sprints. The OS treats overlapping waves from multiple worlds as a super-position that needs to be orchestrated, not a bucket of unrelated tasks.
